question:
Vessel operator Cho is a veteran who has worked on this river for 6 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 223km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 16 units of relief supply box (66kg each) and 5 units of water barrel (22kg each). The boat can travel at 34km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 6km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 20km is Zone A (speed limit: 23km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 21km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (600kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 19% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 25%.
From 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 19%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 48km waypoint, 5 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 89 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 54 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 14 minutes (1st: 54min, 2nd: 68min, 3rd: 82min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

The first 31km is Zone A (speed limit: 30km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 21km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (700kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 21% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 27%.
From 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 19%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 156km waypoint, 6 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 82 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 35 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 12 minutes (1st: 35min, 2nd: 47min, 3rd: 59min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

Ferryman Kim is a veteran who has worked on this river for 7 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 218km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 12 units of relief supply box (62kg each) and 8 units of construction material (70kg each). The boat can travel at 39km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 6km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 33km is Zone A (speed limit: 28km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 22km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (800kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 22%.
From 8:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 20%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 169km waypoint, 2 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 88 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 34 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 16 minutes (1st: 34min, 2nd: 50min, 3rd: 66min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 12 units of construction material (60kg each) and 7 units of relief supply box (35kg each). The boat can travel at 38km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 6km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 31km is Zone A (speed limit: 32km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (600kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 17% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 25%.
From 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 19%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 144km waypoint, 2 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 82 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 47 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 12 minutes (1st: 47min, 2nd: 59min, 3rd: 71min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 18 units of relief supply box (62kg each) and 3 units of construction material (68kg each). The boat can travel at 31km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 6km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 33km is Zone A (speed limit: 21km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 20km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (900kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 18% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 23%.
From 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 20%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 108km waypoint, 1 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 90 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 50 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 16 minutes (1st: 50min, 2nd: 66min, 3rd: 82min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

From 11:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 22%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 168km waypoint, 2 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 83 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 52 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 14 minutes (1st: 52min, 2nd: 66min, 3rd: 80min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
Cargo transporter Park is a veteran who has worked on this river for 10 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 202km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 16 units of construction material (68kg each) and 4 units of water barrel (20kg each). The boat can travel at 38km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 6km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 29km is Zone A (speed limit: 30km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (600kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 20% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 26%.
From 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 19%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 120km waypoint, 2 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 94 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 39 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 12 minutes (1st: 39min, 2nd: 51min, 3rd: 63min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

Freight captain Han is a veteran who has worked on this river for 13 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 222km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 18 units of construction material (53kg each) and 4 units of medical kit (11kg each). The boat can travel at 40km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 6km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 27km is Zone A (speed limit: 32km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 20km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (900kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 13% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 26%.
From 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 19%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 198km waypoint, 7 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 83 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 53 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 13 minutes (1st: 53min, 2nd: 66min, 3rd: 79min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
River courier Seo is a veteran who has worked on this river for 11 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 204km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 18 units of water barrel (18kg each) and 4 units of construction material (73kg each). The boat can travel at 32km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 5km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 33km is Zone A (speed limit: 22km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 20km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (600kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 21% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 22%.
From 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 19%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 132km waypoint, 6 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 84 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 47 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 15 minutes (1st: 47min, 2nd: 62min, 3rd: 77min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

The river has a current of 6km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 24km is Zone A (speed limit: 29km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (900kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 14% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 21%.
From 10:00 AM to 12:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 19%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 65km waypoint, 3 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 82 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 44 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 16 minutes (1st: 44min, 2nd: 60min, 3rd: 76min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

From 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 21%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 91km waypoint, 6 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 83 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 43 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 15 minutes (1st: 43min, 2nd: 58min, 3rd: 73min, ...).

The first 20km is Zone A (speed limit: 23km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (800kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 25%.
From 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 22%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 36km waypoint, 1 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 94 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 39 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 13 minutes (1st: 39min, 2nd: 52min, 3rd: 65min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

### 2. Travel Calculations
*   **Segment 1 (0 to 12 km):** Speed = 19.32 km/h.
    $\text{Time} = \frac{12}{19.32} \approx 0.6211 \text{ hours} \approx 37.27 \text{ minutes}$.
*   **Segment 2 (12 to 20 km):**

Freight captain Han is a veteran who has worked on this river for 15 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 220km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 15 units of construction material (79kg each) and 3 units of water barrel (16kg each). The boat can travel at 28km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 5km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 31km is Zone A (speed limit: 21km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (800kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 21% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 24%.
From 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 19%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 104km waypoint, 2 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 88 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 50 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 12 minutes (1st: 50min, 2nd: 62min, 3rd: 74min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

        2756 / 60 = 45 hours 56 minutes.To find the total travel time for Marine operator Choi, we will calculate the travel time for each segment of the journey, accounting for speed limits, cargo regulations, congestion, and mandatory rest stops.

### 1. Initial Cargo and Speed Limits
*   **Initial Cargo Weight:** (16 boxes × 51 kg) + (4 barrels × 23 kg) = 816 + 92 = 908 kg.
*   **Safety Threshold:** 800 kg. Since 908 > 800, cargo penalties apply.
*   **Zone A Speed Limit:** 33 km/h × (1 - 0.13) = 28.71 km/h.
*   **Zone B Speed Limit:** 19 km/h × (1 - 0.24) = 14.44 km/h.
*   **Zone B Congestion Speed Limit (8:00 AM – 11:00 AM):** 14.44 km/h × (1 - 0.23) = 11.1188 km/h.

### 2. Travel Calculations
*   **Zone A (0 to 21 km):**
    Time = (21 km / 28.71 km/h) × 60 = 43.887 minutes.
*   **Zone B (21 to 24 km):**
    Time = (3 km / 14.44 km/h) × 60 = 12.469 minutes.
    *Cumulative operation time at 24 km = 43.887 + 12.469 = 56.356 min.*
    Since the limit is 94 min, he can reach the 24 km rest point. He must rest here because the next segment (24 to 36 km) would exceed the 94-minute limit.
    **Rest 1 (at 24 km):** 44 minutes.
*   **Zone B (24 to 36 km):**
    Departure time is after 8:00 AM, so congestion speed applies.
    Time = (12 km / 11.1188 km/h) × 60 = 64.752 minutes.
    **Rest 2 (at 36 km):** 56 minutes.
*   **Zone B (36 to 48 km):**
    Congestion ends at 11:00 AM.
    Time from 36 km to 11:00 AM: 11:00 AM - (Departure from 36 km) = 18.892 minutes.
    Distance covered = (18.892 / 60) × 11.1188 = 3.499 km.
    Remaining distance = 12 - 3.499 = 8.501 km.
    Time for remaining distance = (8.501 / 14.44) × 60 = 35.325 minutes.
    Total time for segment = 18.892 + 35.325 = 54.217 minutes.
    **Rest 3 (at 48 km):** 68 minutes.
*   **Zone B (48 to 207 km):**
    Distance = 159 km. Speed = 14.44 km/h.
    Total travel time for this section = (159 / 14.44) × 60 = 660.457 minutes.
    Every 24 km (two 12 km segments), the travel time is (24 / 14.44) × 60 = 99.722 minutes, which exceeds the 94-minute limit. Therefore, he must rest at every 12 km point from 60 km onwards.
    Rest points: 60, 72, 84, 96, 108, 120, 132, 144, 156, 168, 180, 192. (Total 12 more rests).
    *Note: At 168 km, 1 barrel is unloaded, but the weight (885 kg) still exceeds 800 kg, so the speed limit remains 14.44 km/h.*
    *From 192 km to 207 km, the distance is 15 km. Time = (15 / 14.44) × 60 = 62.327 min, which is within the 94-minute limit.*

### 3. Total Time Summation
*   **Total Travel Time:** 43.887 + 12.469 + 64.752 + 54.217 + 660.457 = 835.782 minutes.
*   **Total Rest Time:**
    R1: 44, R2: 56, R3: 68, R4: 80, R5: 92, R6: 104, R7: 116, R8: 128, R9: 140, R10: 152, R11: 164, R12: 176, R13: 188, R14: 200, R15: 212.
    Sum = (15 / 2) × (44 + 212) = 7.5 × 256 = 1920 minutes.
*   **Total Time:** 835.782 + 1920 = 2755.782 minutes.
    Rounding to the nearest integer: 2756 minutes.

### 4. Conversion to Hours and Minutes
2756 minutes = 45 hours and 56 minutes.

Answer: 45 hours 56 minutes

    *   **Rest 2 (at 39 km):** 67 minutes.

Answer: 49 hours 47 minutes

He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 14 units of relief supply box (41kg each) and 3 units of water barrel (16kg each). The boat can travel at 35km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 6km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 20km is Zone A (speed limit: 24km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 20km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (600kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 13% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 27%.
From 11:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 26%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 156km waypoint, 9 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 85 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 55 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 16 minutes (1st: 55min, 2nd: 71min, 3rd: 87min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

River courier Seo is a veteran who has worked on this river for 9 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 199km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 15 units of construction material (65kg each) and 8 units of water barrel (17kg each). The boat can travel at 38km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 5km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 23km is Zone A (speed limit: 29km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (600kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 20% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 24%.
From 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 19%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 120km waypoint, 5 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 84 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 32 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 12 minutes (1st: 32min, 2nd: 44min, 3rd: 56min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 17 units of relief supply box (40kg each) and 7 units of construction material (57kg each). The boat can travel at 38km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 5km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 24km is Zone A (speed limit: 31km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 21km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (600kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 14% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 25%.
From 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 25%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 168km waypoint, 3 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 88 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 34 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 13 minutes (1st: 34min, 2nd: 47min, 3rd: 60min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

- Zone B Congestion Speed Limit (9:00 AM - 11:00 AM): $15.75 \times (1 - 0.25) = 11.8125 \text{ km/h}$

  Remaining distance (1.22 km) at normal speed: $(1.22 / 15.75) \times 60 \approx 4.65 \text{ minutes}$.
  Total travel time: $54.83 + 4.65 = 59.48 \text{ minutes}$.
  Rest 3 (at 48 km): 60 minutes.
  Total elapsed time: $185.31 + 59.48 + 60 = 304.79 \text{ minutes}$. (Arrival: 11:05 AM, Departure: 12:05 PM)

- **48 to 168 km (Zone B):**
  Distance = 120 km (10 segments of 12 km).

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (700kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 23% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 27%.
From 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 18%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 89 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 52 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 13 minutes (1st: 52min, 2nd: 65min, 3rd: 78min, ...).

From 10:00 AM to 2:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 18%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 39km waypoint, 3 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 85 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 48 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 15 minutes (1st: 48min, 2nd: 63min, 3rd: 78min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

The first 25km is Zone A (speed limit: 22km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 21km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (700kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 22% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 25%.
From 8:00 AM to 10:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 23%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 91km waypoint, 1 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 96 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 40 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 13 minutes (1st: 40min, 2nd: 53min, 3rd: 66min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 84 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 39 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 12 minutes (1st: 39min, 2nd: 51min, 3rd: 63min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

Cargo transporter Park is a veteran who has worked on this river for 12 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 214km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 11 units of construction material (51kg each) and 5 units of relief supply box (62kg each). The boat can travel at 44km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 6km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 20km is Zone A (speed limit: 33km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (600kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 20% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 19%.
From 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 24%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 26km waypoint, 1 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 83 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 36 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 12 minutes (1st: 36min, 2nd: 48min, 3rd: 60min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (600kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 20% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 25%.
From 8:00 AM to 10:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 21%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 52km waypoint, 2 units of relief supply box are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 87 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 51 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 12 minutes (1st: 51min, 2nd: 63min, 3rd: 75min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

Freight captain Han is a veteran who has worked on this river for 14 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 227km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 11 units of medical kit (9kg each) and 8 units of construction material (56kg each). The boat can travel at 35km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 6km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 30km is Zone A (speed limit: 28km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (800kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 20% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 22%.
From 10:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 26%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 117km waypoint, 6 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 82 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 32 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 14 minutes (1st: 32min, 2nd: 46min, 3rd: 60min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
Inland navigator Jung is a veteran who has worked on this river for 10 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 215km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 17 units of relief supply box (56kg each) and 3 units of construction material (63kg each). The boat can travel at 33km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 5km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 29km is Zone A (speed limit: 25km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (900kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 15% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 25%.
From 8:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 25%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 144km waypoint, 2 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 85 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 33 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 16 minutes (1st: 33min, 2nd: 49min, 3rd: 65min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

    *   Zone A (Upstream): Effective speed = 33 - 5 = 28 km/h.

Marine operator Choi is a veteran who has worked on this river for 10 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 211km. He departs at 9:00 AM carrying 15 units of construction material (74kg each) and 3 units of water barrel (18kg each). The boat can travel at 38km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 5km/h. In Zone B, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone A, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 18km is Zone A (speed limit: 27km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (800kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 16% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 20%.
From 11:00 AM to 3:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 25%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 130km waypoint, 2 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 89 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 41 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 16 minutes (1st: 41min, 2nd: 57min, 3rd: 73min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

The first 23km is Zone A (speed limit: 24km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 21km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (600kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 22% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 27%.
From 9:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 25%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 91km waypoint, 5 units of water barrel are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 97 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 13km). The base rest duration is 39 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 15 minutes (1st: 39min, 2nd: 54min, 3rd: 69min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

question:
Ferryman Kim is a veteran who has worked on this river for 7 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 221km. He departs at 7:00 AM carrying 16 units of construction material (79kg each) and 5 units of relief supply box (59kg each). The boat can travel at 39km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 5km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 26km is Zone A (speed limit: 31km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (1000kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 18% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 23%.
From 8:00 AM to 11:00 AM is a congestion period, during which the speed limits in all zones are additionally reduced by 25%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 84km waypoint, 5 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 92 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 51 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 14 minutes (1st: 51min, 2nd: 65min, 3rd: 79min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

*   Initial Cargo Weight: $(13 \times 68\text{ kg}) + (3 \times 6\text{ kg}) = 884 + 18 = 902\text{ kg}$.
*   Since $902\text{ kg} > 900\text{ kg}$, speed limits are reduced:

    *   Zone B: $19\text{ km/h} \times (1 - 0.24) = 14.44\text{ km/h}$.
*   Congestion Period (10:00 AM to 12:00 PM) Zone B limit: $14.44\text{ km/h} \times (1 - 0.21) = 11.4076\text{ km/h}$.

**2. Journey Calculations:**
*   **Zone A (0-20 km):**
    *   Effective speed: $\min(36-5, 22.1) = 22.1\text{ km/h}$.
    *   Time: $20\text{ km} / 22.1\text{ km/h} \approx 0.905\text{ hours} \approx 54.30\text{ minutes}$.
    *   Arrival at 20 km: 9:54:18 AM.

*   **Zone B (20-22 km):**
    *   Before 10:00 AM: Time remaining = $5.70\text{ minutes}$. Distance = $14.44 \times (5.70/60) \approx 1.37\text{ km}$.
    *   After 10:00 AM: Remaining distance = $2 - 1.37 = 0.63\text{ km}$. Time = $(0.63 / 11.4076) \times 60 \approx 3.30\text{ minutes}$.
    *   Total time for 20-22 km: $5.70 + 3.30 = 9.00\text{ minutes}$.
    *   Arrival at 22 km: 10:03:18 AM.

*   **Rest Stop 1 (at 22 km):** Duration = $37\text{ minutes}$. (End: 10:40:18 AM)

*   **Zone B (22-33 km):**
    *   Speed: $11.4076\text{ km/h}$ (within congestion period).
    *   Time: $(11 / 11.4076) \times 60 \approx 57.86\text{ minutes}$.
    *   Arrival at 33 km: 11:38:09 AM.

*   **Rest Stop 2 (at 33 km):** Duration = $52\text{ minutes}$. (End: 12:30:09 PM)

*   **Zone B (33-187 km):**
    *   Congestion period has ended. Speed: $14.44\text{ km/h}$.
    *   Distance: $187 - 33 = 154\text{ km}$ (14 segments of 11 km).
    *   Travel time per segment: $(11 / 14.44) \times 60 \approx 45.71\text{ minutes}$.
    *   Total travel time: $14 \times 45.71 \approx 639.89\text{ minutes}$.
    *   Rest stops (Stops 3 to 15): Arithmetic progression from 67 to 247.
    *   Total rest time: $(13/2) \times (67 + 247) = 2041\text{ minutes}$.

*   **Zone B (187-209 km):**
    *   Cargo unloaded: $902 - (2 \times 6) = 890\text{ kg}$.
    *   New speed limit: $19\text{ km/h}$ (no reduction as weight $\le 900\text{ kg}$).
    *   Rest Stop 16 (at 187 km): Duration = $37 + (15 \times 15) = 262\text{ minutes}$.
    *   Travel time: $(22 / 19) \times 60 \approx 69.47\text{ minutes}$.

**3. Total Time Summation:**
Total = $54.30 + 9.00 + 37 + 57.86 + 52 + 639.89 + 2041 + 262 + 69.47 = 3222.52\text{ minutes}$.
Rounding to the nearest integer: $3223\text{ minutes}$.

**4. Conversion to Hours and Minutes:**
$3223\text{ minutes} = 53\text{ hours and } 43\text{ minutes}$.

Answer: 53 hours 43 minutes

Vessel operator Cho is a veteran who has worked on this river for 10 years and has been assigned to transport goods to an upstream region spanning a total of 226km. He departs at 8:00 AM carrying 17 units of construction material (68kg each) and 5 units of medical kit (12kg each). The boat can travel at 44km/h in still water.
The river has a current of 5km/h. In Zone A, the current is downstream (effective speed = boat speed + current speed), while in Zone B, the current is upstream (effective speed = boat speed - current speed).
The first 26km is Zone A (speed limit: 32km/h), followed by Zone B (speed limit: 19km/h). Speed limits apply to the effective speed after accounting for the current.
If the cargo exceeds the safety weight threshold (900kg), the Zone A speed limit is reduced by 18% and the Zone B speed limit is reduced by 27%.
From 9:00 AM to 12:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 24%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 154km waypoint, 5 units of construction material are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 89 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 11km). The base rest duration is 42 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 15 minutes (1st: 42min, 2nd: 57min, 3rd: 72min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)

From 9:00 AM to 1:00 PM is a congestion period, during which the speed limit in Zone B is additionally reduced by 18%. This reduction is applied on top of the speed limit after the cargo regulation has been applied.
At the 132km waypoint, 2 units of medical kit are to be unloaded. After unloading, the cargo regulation is reapplied based on the remaining cargo weight.
The boat cannot operate continuously for more than 93 minutes. Rest is only permitted at designated rest points (every 12km). The base rest duration is 37 minutes. However, due to cumulative fatigue regulations, each rest period increases by 16 minutes (1st: 37min, 2nd: 53min, 3rd: 69min, ...).
Following all of the above rules, what is the total travel time including mandatory rest stops to reach the final destination? First calculate the total travel time as an integer number of minutes, then convert to hours and minutes. (e.g., 1450 minutes total → 24 hours 10 minutes)
